bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting maximum utility and holding firm on the high-weight issues. The opponent’s opening bid gives me the minimum valid value on every issue, and their “market-standard” message is cheap talk with no supporting concession history, so I will counter with my strongest valid package.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02, and the opponent has not yet shown any real concession beyond repeating an all-valueE package that is very poor for me. Their messages about authorization and market standards are cheap talk, so I rely on the offer history rather than accepting those claims. To avoid simply repeating my prior all-valueA offer, I make a small concession only on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the most important issues.

I am targeting a very high utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s valid offers remain quite low for me, rising only from about 0.20 to 0.26. Their messages claim limited authority, but their actual offer history shows they did move on issueA, so I treat that as cheap talk and make only modest concessions on my lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility early-round bid around 0.92, since the deadline is still far away at t=0.06 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only conceded slightly on issueA while their messages claim immobility, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and concede first on my lowest-weight issues, issueD and issueE, while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.88 because it is still very early at t=0.08, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only moved slightly from all valueE to limited concessions on issueA or issueB, while their messages claiming “limits” are not reliable; I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues A and B and concede further on lower-weight issues D and E.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.84 because it is still very early at t=0.10, so there is no need to concede on the most important issues yet. The opponent has made small concessions on issueA and issueB despite claiming each offer is final, so I trust the offer history over the messages and will hold firm on issueA/issueB/issueC while conceding the low-weight issueD and issueE.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.80 because it is still early at t=0.12 and the opponent’s offers remain low for me despite slight concessions on issueA and issueB. Their messages repeatedly claim hard limits, but the offer history contradicts that, so I treat those claims as cheap talk and continue conceding mainly on lower/medium-weight issues while protecting issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.76, since it is early at t=0.14 and the opponent’s offers remain much worse for me despite small concessions. The opponent’s “authorization” messages are cheap talk and contradicted by their repeated movement, so I will continue conceding mainly on lower-weight issues while holding firm on high-weight issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72 because it is early in the negotiation, so I should not concede heavily on high-weight issues yet. The opponent’s offers have improved for me somewhat, especially their latest move to issueA: valueA, but their repeated “authorization limit” claims are cheap talk and contradicted by prior movement. I will hold firm on the most important issues A and B while conceding further on lower/mid-weight issues to test whether that attracts movement.

I am targeting about 0.68 utility by holding firm on the two highest-weight issues, issueA and issueB, while conceding further on lower/mid-weight issues. At t=0.18 it is still early, so I should not move close to the opponent’s low-utility packages yet; their repeated “authorization limit” messages are cheap talk, though their offers show some willingness to vary issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a moderate-high utility of about 0.62: I keep my highest-weight issueA at valueA, concede issueB one step to valueB, and leave lower-priority issueC/issueD/issueE at valueE. At t=0.20 there is still time, but the opponent’s offers have gradually improved for me from all valueE to a current utility around 0.44, so I am making a meaningful but not final concession; I treat their repeated “authorization limit” messages as cheap talk contradicted by their actual movement.

I am targeting a utility around 0.62, since it is still early at t=0.22 and there is no need to concede down toward the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent’s offers remain mostly at valueE and their “authorization” messages are inconsistent with their actual movement, so I treat the messages as cheap talk while acknowledging they have shown some flexibility on issueA and limited movement on issueB/issueE. To avoid repeating my previous exact bid, I am swapping the main concession from issueB to issueA while holding firm on the other high-weight issue.

I am targeting about 0.60 utility, which is a meaningful concession from my ideal while still protecting the high-weight issues A and B. At t=0.24 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s low offers; their repeated “authorization limit” messages are contradicted by their actual movement across issueA, issueB, and issueC. Since their latest offer improved issueB but keeps issueD and issueE at valueE, I am conceding on the lower-weight/back-end issues and offering a balanced shift on issueB.

I am targeting about 0.56 utility, which is a meaningful but still acceptable concession at t=0.26 while preserving my strongest issueA at valueA. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat, especially showing willingness on issueA and occasional movement on issueB/issueC, while their repeated “authorization limit” messages are cheap talk contradicted by continued changes. I will concede mainly on issueB and the lower-weight issues while keeping issueA firm.

I targeted a utility a bit above the opponent’s best recent offers while continuing measured concessions at t=0.28, where there is still time to bargain. The opponent’s repeated “authorization limit” messages are contradicted by their changing bids, so I treat them as cheap talk; their offers suggest they strongly prefer lower values, especially on issueB/C and the lower-weight issues, so I concede there while holding issueA.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.62: still above the opponent’s best offers to me so far, but more conciliatory than my early positions given we are at t=0.30. The opponent’s repeated “authorization limit” messages are not credible because their bids have changed over time; their offers suggest they may tolerate issueA at valueA and issueB at valueC, while they strongly prefer low values on issueD/issueE, which are lower-weight for me.

I am targeting about 0.60 utility, conceding meaningfully from my prior bids while still protecting the high-weight issues, especially issueA and issueB. At t=0.32 there is time left, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s latest 0.54-value offer, but their bids show some willingness to move on issueA, issueB, and issueC despite repeated “authorization limit” messages. I therefore keep their latest strong concession on issueA, accept low-value terms on issueD and issueE, and ask for one step better on issueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.58, which is a modest concession from my prior 0.60 while still protecting the highest-weight issueA and getting some movement on issueB. At t=0.34 there is time left, so I do not need to accept the opponent’s latest 0.52-equivalent package, but their recent offers show willingness to give issueA: valueA and issueC: valueC, so I am combining those with a smaller ask on issueB. The opponent’s repeated “authorization limit” messages are not reliable, but the actual offers show gradual concessions.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.66, which improves slightly on the opponent’s latest offer to me while remaining close enough to their recent movement to be plausible. At t=0.36 there is still time, so I am not accepting their exact low-C/D/E package yet, but I am conceding heavily on the lower-weight issues and preserving strong terms on issueA/issueB. The opponent’s repeated “authorization” claims are not reliable, but their actual bids show meaningful movement on issueA and issueB, especially the latest offer.

I am targeting about 0.64 utility: still above the opponent’s best offer to me so far, but with meaningful mid-game concessions on issueB, issueC, issueD, and issueE. At t=0.38 there is pressure to move toward combinations the opponent has signaled, but not enough to drop immediately to their best prior package; their repeated “no discretion” messages are cheap talk and contradicted by their actual concessions.

I am targeting a utility around 0.64, which is a moderate mid-game concession while still protecting the high-weight issues A and B. The opponent’s repeated “authorization limit” messages are cheap talk, since their bids have moved over time; their recent offers show willingness to give issueB: valueA and occasionally improve issueE, so I am combining those signals without accepting their weaker issueA positions. At t=0.40 there is no need to drop to their best prior offer yet, but this bid is closer to packages they have actually signaled than my earlier harder positions.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.68 by holding firm on the two highest-weight issues, issueA and issueB, while conceding issueC, issueD, and issueE fully. The opponent’s offers and repeated “authorization limit” messages are not reliable as binding constraints, but their bids show some willingness to move on issueA and issueB separately, so I am combining those signals while making substantial concessions on lower-weight issues.
